title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
description: The US command says it is conducting more strikes to degrade Iran's armed forces, as Iran reports explosions near the Strait of Hormuz.
content:
The US military says it is carrying out the seventh night of strikes on Iran since US President Donald Trump declared the temporary ceasefire agreement was "over".
"The strikes are designed to continue degrading Iranian military capabilities at the C... [2240 chars]

title: UK weather: More heatwaves possible in second half of summer
description: Forecasts suggest the weather could turn more changeable during late July and August but the risk of heatwaves remains.
content:
The UK's weather may turn a little more changeable during the second half of summer with spells of rain featuring in some long-range forecasts.
There is also a high chance that temperatures will remain largely above average during late July and Augus... [3137 chars]

title: Gracie Abrams' Daughter from Hell: Rebellion never sounded so toothless
description: The singer's third album is full of incisive, witty lyrics that are let down by muted production.
content:
Knives. So many knives. Gracie Abrams' third album is like a cutlery drawer full of daggers.
They're stuck in her back, waiting to be twisted by a two-faced friend. She's "cut to the bone" by the careless words of a lover. Abrams even has a song call... [5337 chars]

title: First Night of the Proms review - 250th anniversary of US independence takes centre stage
description: From Copland to Gershwin and a new Emily Dickinson-based commission it was America’s evening – but with a surprise Mancunian encore
content: And we’re back. The “world’s greatest classical music festival” has flung wide the doors of the Royal Albert Hall for another eight-week season. Where the Last Night of the Proms is often strangely separate – a self-contained musical party for an ent... [2579 chars]

title: Could these twins' rare genetic disorder provide the key to preventing cancer?
description: Researchers have found that people with Laron syndrome have a lower incidence of cancer than the wider population.
content:
Nestled in southern Ecuador's network of Andes mountains is the town of Piñas, whose 8,000 inhabitants live in houses scattered across a valley.
This remote town is home to an unusually high number of people living with Laron syndrome, a rare genetic... [6961 chars]
